description: Deep causal chain: Terraform phantom in-place updates caused by schema-sensitive attributes not applied to provider response values

required_findings.description: Identifies node_resource_abstract_instance.go as where provider responses (ReadResource/PlanResourceChange) are processed
Identifies that provider responses come back WITHOUT sensitivity marks — providers don't mark values as sensitive, Terraform must add marks
Identifies that only prior state marks (from sensitive variable references) were re-applied, missing schema-defined sensitive attributes
Identifies evaluate.go or GetResource as the only place that applied schema marks, creating an asymmetry
Explains that sensitive_attributes in state was incomplete — missing schema-defined sensitive paths
Identifies marks.go or markProviderSensitiveAttributes as the sensitivity mark application mechanism
Identifies AttrSensitivePaths or the state storage structure for sensitivity paths
Explains that the phantom diff arises from comparing values with different sensitivity marks (not different attribute values)
Mentions ReadResource or PlanResourceChange as the provider protocol calls that return unmarked values
Identifies unmarkValueForStorage or the mechanism that extracts marks before writing to state file
Mentions the JSON output issue — terraform show -json produces incomplete sensitive_values

file_references.description: Identifies internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go (where provider response is processed in refresh/plan)
Identifies internal/terraform/evaluate.go (where schema marks were applied only during expression evaluation)
Identifies internal/states/instance_object.go (state serialization with sensitive paths)
Identifies internal/command/ or JSON output files (terraform show -json output)
Identifies provider schema or provider interaction boundary (internal/providers/ or configs/configschema/)

causal_chain.description: Hop 1: Provider's ReadResource/PlanResourceChange returns instance value WITHOUT sensitivity marks (providers don't add marks)
Hop 2: node_resource_abstract_instance.go re-applies only prior-state sensitivity marks (from sensitive variable references), NOT schema-defined sensitive attributes
Hop 3: State serialization (instance_object.go / AttrSensitivePaths) stores incomplete sensitivity — only variable-reference marks, not schema marks
Hop 4: evaluate.go's GetResource was the only place adding schema marks, so values from state get marks that values stored in state never had — this asymmetry causes phantom diffs in plan comparison

negative_checks.description: Does NOT blame the provider implementation or provider plugin protocol as the root cause
Does NOT claim the bug is in the plan diffing algorithm itself or in the graph walker's traversal order
